title: Appearance Lifecycle Wiring — Room Enter/Leave + Cache Warming

changes.title: Room leave cleanup on room switch
changes.description: Before joining a new room, _doJoinRoom now calls cleanupRoomAppearance(previousRoom) when switching rooms. This ensures that appearance assets (style tags, particle canvases, observers) from the previous room are properly torn down before loading the next room's appearance. Currently a diagnostic stub — full teardown logic activates in v1.0.0.10.

changes.title: Room appearance loaded on join
changes.description: _doJoinRoom now fires loadRoomAppearance(room).then(() => applyRoomAppearance(room)) as a fire-and-forget call after the existing loadRoomSettings + _applyRoomBg sequence. Room appearance is TTL-gated (60 s) and safe to call on every join. Full application logic activates in v1.0.0.10.

changes.title: Stale refresh queues appearance warming
changes.description: _refreshStaleProfilesInView (called every 30 s in the poll loop) now also queues _maybeLoadUserAppearance(uid) for every visible sender, staggered at 200 ms intervals to spread GitHub API calls. Calls are TTL-gated so no duplicate fetches occur within 5 minutes.

changes.title: createMessageElement warms appearance cache
changes.description: createMessageElement now calls _maybeLoadUserAppearance(m.userId) as a fire-and-forget for remote senders when a message is built. The loader is TTL-gated and returns immediately for cached entries, so rendering performance is unaffected. This ensures appearance data is populated before v1.0.0.5+ DOM decoration code runs.
